seoTitle: How to Create Automations in Brevo Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to build a Brevo marketing automation workflow with entry triggers, timed delays, conditional splits, and email actions.
intro: Manually tracking every new lead and sending follow-up emails one by one doesn't scale. This guide shows how to use Brevo's visual workflow editor to build a multi-step automation, starting with an entry trigger like a list signup, adding delays and conditional splits, and finishing with a test before activating it live. It's written for anyone setting up their first welcome sequence or nurture flow.

steps.heading: Start a new automation
steps.body: Go to Automations, then Workflows, and click Create an automation, choosing either a blank layout or a ready-made welcome sequence.

steps.heading: Set your entry point
steps.body: Click Add an entry point and choose what triggers the workflow, such as a contact being added to a specific list.

steps.heading: Add a delay
steps.body: Click the plus icon below your entry node and add a time delay block, such as waiting two days before the next action.

steps.heading: Add an email action and branching logic
steps.body: Add a Send an email step using a saved template, and optionally drag in a conditional split block to route contacts based on behavior like email opens or clicks.

steps.heading: Test and activate
steps.body: Use the Test the workflow tool to verify execution paths, then click Activate automation to launch the sequence live.

faq.question: What can trigger a Brevo automation to start?
faq.answer: Common entry triggers include a contact being added to a list, a profile attribute update, or a custom website event.

faq.question: Can I test my automation before it goes live?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Test the workflow tool in the top console ribbon to verify exit conditions and pathways before activating.

faq.question: Can automations send more than just emails?
faq.answer: Yes, workflows can combine email dispatches, SMS campaigns, and CRM task creation within the same sequence.

faq.question: How do I branch contacts down different paths in an automation?
faq.answer: Use a conditional split block to route users based on behaviors like whether they opened a specific email or clicked a link.
